proposed_hypotheses.text: Higher values of feature_078 (continuous, range 30-90, mean 65) are associated with longer pfs_months.

analyses.result_summary: Spearman correlation between feature_078 and pfs_months is rho=0.785 (p<1e-300). Mean PFS rises monotonically across deciles of feature_078 from 1.33 (lowest decile) to 7.45 months (highest decile). feature_078 is by far the dominant predictor in univariate analysis.

proposed_hypotheses.text: feature_057 (3-level ordinal: 0/1/2) is negatively associated with pfs_months — each higher level corresponds to shorter PFS.

analyses.result_summary: Mean PFS: feature_057=0 -> 5.27 months (n=17592); =1 -> 4.05 months (n=24971); =2 -> 2.91 months (n=7437). One-way ANOVA F=3528.9, p<1e-300. Each step in feature_057 reduces PFS by ~1.2 months. Pattern matches a performance-status-like ordinal variable.

proposed_hypotheses.text: feature_051 (binary, prevalence 55%) is associated with shorter pfs_months in patients with feature_051=1 vs feature_051=0.

analyses.result_summary: feature_051=1 mean PFS = 3.70 (n=27481) vs feature_051=0 mean PFS = 5.05 (n=22519); Welch t-test p<1e-300. Adjusted for feature_078, feature_057, and feature_099, the OLS coefficient is -1.36 (p<1e-300). Plausibly a binary stage/metastatic indicator.

proposed_hypotheses.text: feature_038 (binary, prevalence 20%) is associated with longer pfs_months — patients with feature_038=1 have higher PFS than feature_038=0.

analyses.result_summary: feature_038=1 mean PFS = 5.09 (n=10022) vs feature_038=0 mean PFS = 4.12 (n=39978); Welch t-test p~1.7e-216. After adjustment for feature_078, feature_057, feature_051, and feature_099, coefficient is +0.94 months (p<1e-300). Direction strongly positive.

proposed_hypotheses.text: feature_099 (continuous, range 0-25) is negatively associated with pfs_months.

analyses.result_summary: Spearman rho = -0.120 (p~5.6e-160). After adjustment in OLS for feature_078, feature_057, feature_051, feature_092, the coefficient on feature_099 is -0.076 per unit (p<1e-300). Direction confirmed negative.

proposed_hypotheses.text: feature_092 (continuous, range 1.5-5.5) is positively associated with pfs_months.

analyses.result_summary: Spearman rho = +0.103 (p~4.4e-117). Adjusted OLS coefficient for feature_092 (controlling for feature_078, feature_057, feature_051, feature_099) is +0.46 per unit (p<1e-300). Pattern (range 1.5-5.5, mean 3.8) is consistent with a serum-protein-like measure (e.g., albumin) where higher values mark less-frail patients.

proposed_hypotheses.text: feature_013 (binary, prevalence 42%) is associated with shorter pfs_months in patients with feature_013=1.

analyses.result_summary: feature_013=1 mean PFS = 4.12 (n=20898) vs feature_013=0 mean PFS = 4.45 (n=29102); Welch t p~1.9e-58. Adjusted OLS coefficient -0.33 months (p<1e-300). Modest main effect but, as later iterations show, this variable strongly modifies the feature_038 treatment effect.

proposed_hypotheses.text: feature_043 (binary, prevalence 35%) is associated with shorter pfs_months in patients with feature_043=1.

analyses.result_summary: feature_043=1 mean PFS = 4.11 (n=17657) vs feature_043=0 mean PFS = 4.42 (n=32343); Welch t p~1.0e-49. Adjusted OLS coefficient -0.29 months (p<1e-300). Like feature_013, modest main effect that hides strong interaction.

proposed_hypotheses.text: feature_109 (binary, prevalence 4.5%) is associated with shorter pfs_months in patients with feature_109=1.

analyses.result_summary: feature_109=1 mean PFS = 4.09 (n=2272) vs feature_109=0 mean PFS = 4.32 (n=47728); Welch t p~3.5e-7. Adjusted OLS coefficient is -0.36 (p<1e-30). Small main effect, but again interacts strongly with feature_038.

proposed_hypotheses.text: feature_009 (continuous, right-skewed, range 0.02-777) is weakly negatively associated with pfs_months.

analyses.result_summary: Spearman rho = -0.047 (p~1.3e-25). In multivariable OLS adjusted for feature_078, feature_057, feature_051, feature_099, the coefficient on feature_009 is -0.0051 per unit (p~2.5e-75). Direction negative, magnitude small.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Even after joint adjustment, the top univariate predictors (feature_078, feature_057, feature_051, feature_038, feature_099, feature_092, feature_013, feature_043, feature_009, feature_109) each retain a statistically significant independent effect on pfs_months in the same direction as their univariate association.

analyses.code: smf.ols('pfs_months ~ feature_078 + C(feature_057) + feature_051 + feature_038 + feature_099 + feature_092 + feature_013 + feature_043 + feature_009 + feature_109', data=df).fit()
analyses.result_summary: Joint OLS R^2 = 0.858 (adj R^2 = 0.858). All ten predictors retain p<1e-15 in the same direction as their univariate association: feature_078 +0.176/unit, feature_057[1] -1.20, feature_057[2] -2.33, feature_051 -1.36, feature_038 +0.94, feature_099 -0.076, feature_092 +0.47, feature_013 -0.35, feature_043 -0.29, feature_009 -0.005, feature_109 -0.36. None are explained away by another predictor.

proposed_hypotheses.text: feature_038 effect on pfs_months is modified by feature_013: the PFS benefit of feature_038=1 is large among patients with feature_013=0 but absent among those with feature_013=1 (negative interaction).

analyses.code: smf.ols('pfs_months ~ feature_038 * feature_013 + feature_078 + C(feature_057) + feature_051 + feature_099', data=df).fit()
analyses.result_summary: In feature_013=0 stratum: feature_038=1 mean PFS 5.77 vs feature_038=0 mean 4.12 (diff +1.66 months, n=29102). In feature_013=1 stratum: 4.14 vs 4.12 (diff +0.03 months, n=20898). OLS interaction term feature_038:feature_013 = -1.78 (p<1e-300). Strong negative interaction confirmed.

proposed_hypotheses.text: feature_038 effect on pfs_months is modified by feature_043 in the same direction as h12: large benefit when feature_043=0, no benefit when feature_043=1.

analyses.result_summary: In feature_043=0 stratum: feature_038=1 mean 5.60 vs feature_038=0 mean 4.12 (diff +1.48 months, n=32343). In feature_043=1 stratum: 4.14 vs 4.11 (diff +0.03 months, n=17657). OLS interaction feature_038:feature_043 = -1.47 (p<1e-300). Pattern parallels feature_013.

proposed_hypotheses.text: feature_038 effect on pfs_months is also nullified (and possibly reversed) when feature_109=1 — same biomarker-resistance pattern.

analyses.result_summary: In feature_109=0 stratum: feature_038=1 mean 5.14 vs feature_038=0 mean 4.12 (diff +1.03, n=47728). In feature_109=1 stratum: feature_038=1 mean 3.97 vs feature_038=0 mean 4.13 (diff -0.16, n=2272). OLS interaction feature_038:feature_109 = -1.76 (p~1.3e-88). Strong negative interaction; treatment benefit completely abrogated and possibly reversed in the small feature_109+ group.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Unlike feature_013/043/109, feature_067 (binary, prevalence 3%) does NOT abrogate the feature_038 effect — if anything, the feature_038 benefit is larger when feature_067=1 (positive interaction).

analyses.result_summary: In feature_067=0 stratum: feature_038=1 mean 5.06 vs feature_038=0 mean 4.12 (diff +0.94, n=48494). In feature_067=1 stratum: feature_038=1 mean 6.05 vs feature_038=0 mean 4.13 (diff +1.93, n=1506). Univariate stratified interaction term ~+0.89 (p~3.2e-50). After joint adjustment for feature_013/043/109 the residual feature_038:feature_067 interaction shrinks to +0.07 and is no longer significant (p=0.15) — consistent with feature_067 marking patients enriched for absence of the resistance markers.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Defining 'resistance-marker negative' as feature_013=0 AND feature_043=0 AND feature_109=0, feature_038 produces a much larger pfs_months benefit (>2 months) in this subgroup than in the rest of the cohort (~0 months).

analyses.result_summary: Triple-marker-negative subgroup (n=17372): feature_038=1 mean 6.88 vs feature_038=0 mean 4.12 (diff +2.76 months, p<1e-300). Anyone with at least one positive marker (n=32628): feature_038=1 mean 4.15 vs feature_038=0 mean 4.12 (diff +0.03 months, p=0.29). Treatment benefit is essentially confined to the resistance-marker-negative subgroup. The pattern is the canonical biomarker-treatment-interaction signature seen in oncology (e.g., anti-EGFR therapy in RAS/BRAF wild-type colorectal cancer).

proposed_hypotheses.text: Mean pfs_months differs across categories of feature_064 (race: white, black, hispanic, asian, other).

analyses.result_summary: Group means: white 4.316 (n=32456), hispanic 4.315 (n=7572), asian 4.306 (n=3003), black 4.273 (n=5996), other 4.407 (n=973). One-way ANOVA F=0.87, p=0.48. No detectable raw association between race (feature_064) and pfs_months. Hypothesis NOT supported.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Mean pfs_months differs across categories of feature_018 (insurance: medicare, private, medicaid, uninsured).

analyses.result_summary: Group means: medicare 4.312 (n=21105), private 4.330 (n=19898), medicaid 4.277 (n=6991), uninsured 4.249 (n=2006). One-way ANOVA F=1.48, p=0.22. No detectable association between insurance type (feature_018) and pfs_months. Hypothesis NOT supported.

proposed_hypotheses.text: The probability of receiving feature_038 (the apparent treatment) differs across racial groups (feature_064).

analyses.result_summary: Proportion receiving feature_038=1 by race: white 20.0%, hispanic 19.8%, black 20.1%, asian 21.1%, other 19.2%. Chi-square test of independence p=0.55. No evidence of disparity in feature_038 receipt by race. Hypothesis NOT supported.

proposed_hypotheses.text: The probability of receiving feature_038 differs across insurance categories (feature_018).

analyses.result_summary: Proportion receiving feature_038=1 by insurance: medicare 20.2%, private 20.1%, medicaid 19.8%, uninsured 19.0%. Chi-square test of independence p=0.59. No evidence of disparity in feature_038 receipt by insurance. Hypothesis NOT supported.

proposed_hypotheses.text: The pfs_months benefit from feature_038 differs across racial groups (feature_064 modifies the feature_038 effect).

analyses.code: smf.ols('pfs_months ~ feature_038*C(feature_064) + feature_078 + C(feature_057) + feature_051 + feature_099', data=df).fit()
analyses.result_summary: Stratified PFS gain on feature_038: white +0.92, black +0.97, hispanic +1.11, asian +1.09, other +1.22. All four feature_038 x C(feature_064) interaction terms are non-significant (p between 0.10 and 0.88). Hypothesis NOT supported — treatment benefit is uniform across races.

proposed_hypotheses.text: The pfs_months benefit from feature_038 differs across insurance categories (feature_018 modifies the feature_038 effect).

analyses.code: smf.ols('pfs_months ~ feature_038*C(feature_018) + feature_078 + C(feature_057) + feature_051 + feature_099', data=df).fit()
analyses.result_summary: Most feature_038 x C(feature_018) interaction terms are null (medicare p=0.16, private p=0.43, medicaid baseline). For uninsured the feature_038 effect is -0.12 months smaller than for medicaid (p=0.044), borderline. Overall, treatment benefit is essentially uniform across insurance categories. Hypothesis essentially NOT supported.

proposed_hypotheses.text: The pfs_months benefit from feature_038 differs across feature_057 (performance-status-like) levels.

analyses.code: smf.ols('pfs_months ~ feature_038*C(feature_057) + feature_078 + feature_051 + feature_099', data=df).fit()
analyses.result_summary: Stratified gain on feature_038: feature_057=0 +0.93, feature_057=1 +1.00, feature_057=2 +0.90. Interaction terms are non-significant. Treatment benefit is essentially uniform across feature_057 levels. Hypothesis NOT supported.

proposed_hypotheses.text: feature_092 (continuous lab-like) modifies the feature_038 effect: the absolute PFS benefit is smaller for higher feature_092 values (negative interaction).

analyses.code: smf.ols('pfs_months ~ feature_038*feature_092 + feature_078 + C(feature_057) + feature_051 + feature_099', data=df).fit()
analyses.result_summary: feature_038:feature_092 interaction coefficient = -0.086 per unit feature_092 (p~1.8e-5). The interaction is small in magnitude vs. the biomarker-driven interactions in iterations 12-14, but direction is consistent: treatment benefit is slightly attenuated as feature_092 increases.

proposed_hypotheses.text: A consolidated multivariable model with the major main effects (feature_078, feature_057, feature_051, feature_099, feature_092, feature_009) plus feature_038 and its three biomarker interactions (feature_013, feature_043, feature_109) and adjusting for race and insurance explains substantially more variance than the additive top-10 model and confirms all directional findings of iterations 1-22.

analyses.code: smf.ols('pfs_months ~ feature_078 + C(feature_057) + feature_051 + feature_092 + feature_099 + feature_009 + feature_038*feature_013 + feature_038*feature_043 + feature_038*feature_109 + feature_038*feature_067 + C(feature_064) + C(feature_018)', data=df).fit()
analyses.result_summary: Final consolidated OLS R^2 = 0.897 (adj R^2 = 0.897), up from 0.858 for the additive 10-predictor model. Main-effect signs all match earlier univariate / adjusted analyses. Biomarker interactions feature_038:feature_013, feature_038:feature_043, feature_038:feature_109 all carry large negative coefficients (~-1.5 to -1.8) and p<1e-50; feature_038:feature_067 is small and non-significant after adjustment. Coefficients on race and insurance categories remain null. Conclusion: PFS in this cohort is driven mostly by patient-level factors (feature_078, feature_057, feature_051, feature_092, feature_099) plus a strongly biomarker-modified feature_038 treatment effect; demographic/socioeconomic strata (race, insurance) are not associated with PFS or with treatment benefit.
